data.title: As a user, I can specify a document folder so onboarding reads my existing project docs to pre-fill answers
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Accept an optional --doc-folder parameter. Scan the folder with a file-type guard (skip binary files and files over 200KB, logged). Parse text docs for structured facts (app name, stack signals, WCAG requirements, framework mentions). Update the confidence context object with parsed facts per the S1 schema contract.

**Why**: Users often have PRDs or design docs that already answer onboarding questions. Without this, onboarding asks questions the user has documented. SC6 requires fewer questions when a folder is specified.

**Scope**:
- IN: --doc-folder CLI param parsing, file-type guard (binary/large file skip with logged warning), text document parsing for structured facts (app name, stack, WCAG, framework mentions), confidence context update per S1 schema contract
- OUT: semantic NLP beyond keyword/pattern matching, non-text formats beyond text-layer PDFs, parsing files beyond PRD/design doc style

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running onboarding with --doc-folder scans the specified folder, skips binary and large files (each skip logged with reason), and extracts at least app name, stack, and WCAG level where present in docs
  <- Satisfies: "a pre-flight phase runs before any onboarding questions... an optional document folder is scanned"
- When this story is complete, a large PDF in the document folder is skipped without stalling the session (within 5 seconds of encountering the file)
  <- Satisfies: "an optional document folder is scanned (primarily product/design docs)"
- When this story is complete, at least one fact extracted from a document elevates its dimension's confidence level in the context object
  <- Satisfies: "a confidence context object is built"
- When this story is complete, a dual-run comparison (with vs. without --doc-folder on the same project) shows the doc-folder run has higher confidence on at least one dimension, resulting in at least one question being skipped entirely and at least one being pre-populated
  <- Satisfies: "A user who specifies a document folder containing a PRD is asked strictly fewer open questions"
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for document parsing logic and file-type guard behavior

## Considerations

- [Security] User-specified folder path must be validated (resolved and confirmed to be accessible) before scanning to prevent path traversal
- [Performance] No guard yet on file count or total scan time — deeply nested folders with hundreds of files could exhaust context; add a cap on files scanned per run
- [Reliability] Binary file stall risk — file-type guard must handle encoding errors gracefully (wrap reads in try/except, skip on error)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate when blocked — proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
